author: codex
scope: validator/rule lane; no generated-output commands; no provider-backed post-ingest commands

recommendation.summary: Allow source records to carry source-backed timeline events. The sampled records consistently use a source document or meeting-material source as the subject and a dated board, committee, approval, timetable, or document event as the object. This is analogous to the already accepted project/route/corridor/entity -> event timeline relation and avoids retire/replace churn for correctly shaped source timeline context.
why_not_data_cleanup: The subject endpoint is intentionally the source record, not a mistaken project/entity endpoint, in the sampled rows.
The object endpoint is already an event record with event/date payload fields.
The relation kind has_timeline_event accurately describes source-document timeline context such as approval schedules, board/committee dates, and timetable periods.
Replacing these rows would require inventing less direct relation kinds or dropping useful source-to-event context rather than correcting bad endpoints.

exact_patch_plan.relations_ts_change: In RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES.has_timeline_event.subject, add "source" to the existing subject list. Do not add event, claim, treatment_component, or any other subject kind in this patch.
exact_patch_plan.relations_test_change: In relation helpers > accepts expected endpoint shapes and reports unexpected known shapes, add expect(relationEndpointShapeIssue("has_timeline_event", "source", "event")).toBeUndefined(); and update the negative expected message for has_timeline_event only if the test asserts the full expected-subject string.
non_goals: Do not widen has_timeline_event to event -> event, claim -> event, or treatment_component -> event in this patch.
Do not run materialize, export-jsonl, full validate, canonicalize, identity-review-run, write, ask, schema, ontology-normalize, or provider/profile commands.

inventory_context.method: Targeted bun --eval over readCanonicalRecords() plus relationEndpointShapeIssue(); not full validate.
inventory_context.batch019_pending_materialize_note: The log says relation batch019 retired nine MTA Bridges and Tunnels facility operated_by entity -> entity rows and appended replacements, but those durable inputs are pending materialize. The targeted inventory excluded those nine retired submission ids so this prep does not duplicate serialized conductor work.

top_buckets_after_excluding_batch019_ret.note: Not included in this patch recommendation; event-to-event timeline semantics need separate review.
Not included in this patch recommendation; claim-to-event semantics are less uniform and need separate review.
Previously proposed broader rule gap class, but not part of this narrow patch.

evidence_samples.reason: The source document itself describes a timetable-change period event.

commands_run: bun --eval targeted endpoint-shape inventory over readCanonicalRecords() and relationEndpointShapeIssue(), excluding queued batch019 retire ids
bun --eval sample has_timeline_event source->event/event->event/claim->event buckets from readCanonicalRecords()

jq -c selected evidence blocks from raw/sources/meeting_doc_199066/blocks.jsonl, meeting_doc_102816/blocks.jsonl, meeting_doc_164966/blocks.jsonl, meeting_doc_171001/blocks.jsonl, meeting_doc_112151/blocks.jsonl, and meeting_doc_155616/blocks.jsonl
